lessonTitle: Hidden and One-Off Costs of Starting University
concept: Before you attend a single lecture, you may spend $3,000–$10,000 on costs that almost nobody warns you about.

hook.fact: Priya budgeted perfectly for Year 1. Then the invoice for her visa, deposit, and laptop arrived.
hook.question: She had not budgeted for any of it.

conceptCards.title: Card 1
conceptCards.body: **Startup costs** are the one-off expenses that hit before or during your first weeks at university. Unlike TCA categories, they do not repeat every year — but they must be paid before you even begin.

conceptCards.title: Card 2
conceptCards.body: Application fees add up faster than expected. US university applications cost $50–$90 each. Applying to ten schools is $500–$900 before you have been accepted anywhere. Standardised tests (SAT, ACT, IELTS, TOEFL) add another $100–$250 each.

conceptCards.title: Card 3
conceptCards.body: Visa fees and required health checks apply to most international students. A UK student visa costs £363 plus an Immigration Health Surcharge of £776 per year of study. US student visa (F-1) costs $160 plus the SEVIS fee of $350.

conceptCards.title: Card 4
conceptCards.body: Housing deposits (typically one to two months' rent) must be paid upfront before you move in. First-month rent is often due simultaneously. Together, these can total $1,500–$3,000 before you have unpacked a single box.

conceptCards.title: Card 5
conceptCards.body: Now test this: Technology, bedding, kitchenware, and travel to campus are all startup costs. A necessary laptop upgrade alone can be $500–$1,500. Most students spend $500–$1,500 on household supplies in their first month. Budget for all of it before you

questions.prompt: What makes startup costs different from regular TCA categories?
options: They are smaller than regular costs
They occur only once, before or at the start of university
They are covered by student loans
They only apply to international students
questions.explanation: "Startup costs are one-off expenses — they don't repeat annually like tuition or rent, but must be paid before your studies begin."

questions.statement: Applying to 10 US universities costs less than $200 in application fees.
questions.whyPrompt: Why is that true or false?
questions.explanation: "US university applications typically cost $50–$90 each. Ten applications = $500–$900 in fees alone, before any test registration costs."

questions.prompt: Match each startup cost to its approximate amount:

pairs.left: UK student visa + Immigration Health Surcharge (3

questions.explanation: Review the concept and try again.

questions.prompt: Marcus is moving into a flat with €650/month rent. The landlord requires a 2-month deposit plus first month's rent upfront.
questions.explanation: "2 months deposit = €1,300. First month's rent = €650. Total due before moving in: €1,950."

questions.sentence: "The one-off expenses paid before or at the start of university — including visa fees, deposits, and technology — are collectively called ___."
questions.explanation: Review the concept and try again.

questions.sentence: Complete the sentence: "Even a student with enough savings for their full TCA can face problems because startup costs arrive ___ any loans are disbursed, and must be paid ___ the regular academic year budget begins."
questions.explanation: Review the concept and try again.

questions.scenario: Priya's Year 1 savings plan: "I've saved €26,000 for my TCA. I'm ready."
options: Her TCA estimate is too low
She has not set aside €2,700 for startup costs that arrive before
Her savings are more than enough
Startup costs are covered by universities
questions.explanation: "Priya's TCA savings are solid — but startup costs are separate and arrive first. Without an additional €2,700 buffer, she will need to pull from her TCA savings immediately."

tasks.scenario: Build Kai's startup cost budget for starting at a UK university:
tasks.explanation: "Kai's startup costs total over £6,000 — nearly half a year's living costs — before he has attended a single lecture."

tasks.scenario: Sofia has saved $25,000 for Year 1 at NUS Singapore. Her annual TCA is $38,000.
tasks.explanation: "Sofia has a real $18,800 shortfall for Year 1 alone. Identifying this now — not after arriving in Singapore — gives her time to explore loans, family support, or part-time work before the crisis arrives."

reviewQueueEntry.definition: Startup costs anchors better education money decisions.
reviewQueueEntry.quickRecallPrompt: What does Startup costs include beyond tuition?
